title: Understand decomposing figures to find area
description: Practice decomposing figures into rectangles to find area. Some figures are on grids.
topic_path: Topic D: Applications of area using side lengths of figures
Understand decomposing figures to find area

assessment_items.question_content:
Each small square in the diagram has side lengths of $1$ centimeter.



[[☃ image 1]]



**What is the area of the figure?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] $\text{ square cm}$
hints:
We can break the figure into two rectangles and find the area of each rectangle.


[[☃ image 1]]

Let's find the area of the first rectangle.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\blueD{\text{Area}} &=\blueD 6 \times \blueD2\\
&= \blueD {12} \text { square cm}\end{align}$

Now, let's find the area of the second rectangle.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\maroonD{\text{Area}} &=\maroonD 2 \times \maroonD5\\
&= \maroonD {10} \text { square cm}\end{align}$

Last, we can add the areas of each rectangle to find the area of the whole figure.

$\blueD{12}\text{ square cm}+\maroonD{10}\text{ square cm}=22\text{ square cm}$

We can also count the square centimeters that cover the figure.



[[☃ image 1]]
The area of the figure is $22\text{ square cm}$.

options.alt: A figure composed of two rectangles shown a grid of squares. The top rectangle has a length of 6 squares and a width of 2 squares. The bottom rectangle has a length of 2 squares and a width of 5 squares.

assessment_items.question_content:

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ input-number 1]] $\text{ square cm}$
hints:

[[☃ image 1]]

Let's find the area of the first rectangle.

 
[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\blueD{\text{Area}} &=\blueD7 \times \blueD2\\
&= \blueD{14} \text { square cm}\end{align}$

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\maroonD{\text{Area}} &=\maroonD5 \times \maroonD3\\
&= \maroonD{15} \text { square cm}\end{align}$

Last, we can add the areas of each rectangle to find the area of the whole figure.

$\blueD{14} \text{ square cm} + \maroonD{15} \text{ square cm} = 29 \text{ square cm}$

We can also count the square centimeters that cover the figure.


[[☃ image 1]]
The area of the figure is $29 \text{ square cm}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each small square in the diagram has side lengths of $1$ meter.


[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the figure?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] $\text{ square meters}$
hints:
We can break the figure into two rectangles and find the area of each rectangle.


[[☃ image 1]]

Let's find the area of the first rectangle.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\blueD{\text{Area}} &=\blueD5 \times \blueD4\\
&= \blueD{20} \text { square m}\end{align}$

Now, let's find the area of the second rectangle.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}\maroonD{\text{Area}} &=\maroonD2 \times \maroonD3\\
&= \maroonD{6} \text { square m}\end{align}$

Last, we can add the areas of each rectangle to find the area of the whole figure.

$\blueD{20}\text{ square m}+\maroonD{6}\text{ square m}=26\text{ square m}$

We can also count the square meters that cover the figure.



[[☃ image 1]]
The area of the figure is $26\text{ square meters}$.

options.alt: A six sided figure made of two rectangles shown on a grid made of unit squares. The left rectangle has a length of 4 squares and a width of 5 squares. The right rectangle has a length of 2 squares and a width of 3 squares.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below shows a large rectangle with a small rectangle cut out of it.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the area of the shaded figure by subtracting the areas.**  

||
-: | :-
$\text{Area }$ |$=54-{}$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]] 
|$=$ [[☃ numeric-input 3]]$\text{ square units}$
hints:
We can think of the figure as a large rectangle with a small rectangular area removed.

$\underbrace{\text{Area}}_{\text{shaded figure}} = \underbrace{\text{Area}}_{\text{large rectangle}} - \underbrace{\text{Area}}_{\text{small rectangle}}$

[[☃ image 1]]

The area of each rectangle is the base times the height.

First, let's find the area of the large rectangle.


[[☃ image 1]]

  
$\begin{align}
\blueE{\text{Area}} &=\blueE{6} \times \blueE{9}\\
&= \blueE{54} \text { square units}\end{align}$

Next, let's find the area of the small rectangle cut out.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}\maroonE{\text{Area}} &=\maroonE{3} \times \maroonE{4}\\
&= \maroonE{12} \text { square units}\end{align}$

options.alt: An 8-sided figure in the shape of a large rectangle with a small rectangle cut out from its left side. The large rectangle is 6 units long and 9 units wide.   The space left from the small rectangle is 3 units long and 4 units wide.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below is made of $2$ rectangles.


[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the area of the shaded figure by adding the areas.**  

$\text{Area }=$ [[☃ numeric-input 3]] $+$ $8 =$ [[☃ numeric-input 4]]$\text{ square meters}$
hints:
We can break the figure into two rectangles and find the area of each rectangle.



[[☃ image 1]]

Let's find the area of the first rectangle.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\blueD{\text{Area}} &=\blueD 3 \times \blueD7\\
&= \blueD {21} \text { square m}\end{align}$

Next, let's find the area of the second rectangle.    



[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}\maroonD{\text{Area}} &=\maroonD 4 \times \maroonD2\\
&= \maroonD {8} \text { square m}\end{align}$

Last, we can add the areas of each rectangle to find the area of the whole figure.

$\text{Area }=\blueD {21}  + \maroonD {8} = 29 \text{ square m}$

options.alt: A figure composed of two rectangles. The left rectangle has a length of 3 meters and width of 7 meters. The right rectangle has a length of 4 meters and width of 2 meters.

assessment_items.question_content:

[[☃ image 1]]

  

$\begin{align}
\maroonD{\text{Area}} &=\maroonD3 \times \maroonD2\\

Last, we can add the areas of each rectangle to find the area of the whole figure.

$\text{Area }=\blueD {16}  + \maroonD {6} = 22 \text{ square m}$

Let's find the area of the first rectangle.   
   


[[☃ image 1]]

 

$\begin{align}
\blueD{\text{Area}} &=\blueD{1} \times \blueD{5}\\
&= \blueD{5} \text { square cm}\end{align}$

Next, let's find the area of the second rectangle.  



[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\maroonD{\text{Area}} &=\maroonD{6} \times \maroonD{2}\\
&= \maroonD{12} \text { square cm}\end{align}$

Last, we can add the areas of each rectangle to find the area of the whole figure.

$\text{Area }=\blueD{5}+\maroonD{12}=17\text{ square cm}$

options.alt: A figure made of two rectangles. The left rectangle has a length of 1 centimeter and a width of 5 centimeter. The right rectangle has a length of 6 centimeters and a width of 2 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below is made of $2$ rectangles.


[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the area of the shaded figure by adding the areas.**  

$\text{Area }=6$ $+$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]$\text{ square centimeters}$
hints:
We can break the figure into two rectangles and find the area of each rectangle.


[[☃ image 1]]

Let's find the area of the first rectangle.  


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\blueD{\text{Area}} &=\blueD{6} \times \blueD{1}\\
&= \blueD{6} \text { square cm}\end{align}$

Next, let's find the area of the second rectangle.  


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\maroonD{\text{Area}} &=\maroonD{3} \times \maroonD{7}\\
&= \maroonD{21} \text { square cm}\end{align}$

Last, we can add the areas of each rectangle to find the area of the whole figure.

$\text{Area }=\blueD{6}+\maroonD {21}=27\text{ square cm}$

options.alt: A figure composed of two rectangles. The top rectangle has a length of 6 centimeters and a width of 1 centimeter. The bottom rectangle has a length of 3 centimeters and a width of 7 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below is made of $2$ rectangles.


[[☃ image 1]]



**Find the area of the shaded figure by adding the areas.**  

$\text{Area }=10$ $+$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]$\text{ square centimeters}$
hints:
We can break the figure into two rectangles and find the area of each rectangle.


[[☃ image 1]]

Let's find the area of the first rectangle.  
   


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\blueD{\text{Area}} &=\blueD{2} \times \blueD{5}\\
&= \blueD{10} \text { square cm}\end{align}$

Next, let's find the area of the second rectangle.  


[[☃ image 1]]

 

$\begin{align}\maroonD{\text{Area}} &=\maroonD{8} \times \maroonD{3}\\
&= \maroonD{24} \text { square cm}\end{align}$

Last, we can add the areas of each rectangle to find the area of the whole figure.

$\text{Area }=\blueD{10}+\maroonD{24}=34\text{ square cm}$

options.alt: A figure composed of two rectangles. The top rectangle has a length of 2 centimeters and a width of 5 centimeters. The bottom rectangle has a length of 8 centimeters and a width of 3 centimeters.
